plain_language: Today: Microsoft Agent 365 GA, Pentagon-Anthropic split, S&P record close. Agent control-plane and AI-infra security predictions confirm strongly. Apr Jobs print moves from May 1 to May 8.
findings:
Today's report shows the strongest single-day confirmation signal of the past week against the agent-control-plane and AI-Infra-CVE-class predictions. Microsoft Agent 365 GA on May 1 — at $15/user/month inside M365 E7, with tenant-level allow/block for MCP servers + Entra/Purview/Intune/Defender integration — is the literal "MCP Gateway / MCP Firewall" enterprise primitive the 2026-04-25 prediction names, and it lands four months ahead of that prediction's Q3 window. Combined with the May 1 CSA "Identity in the Age of AI" publication and the carry-forward CSAI Foundation MITRE-CNA + ATT&CK v19 named-techniques wave, the entire MCP-protocol-attack-surface / agent-skills threat-matrix / AI-Infra-CVE-class cluster is materializing on schedule.

The KV-cache-compression-as-first-class-knob predictions (2026-04-29 and 2026-04-30) are the second-strongest cluster: today adds CodeComp / CacheFlow / TokenDance / TTKV to the existing TurboQuant + PolyKV + kvpress + Unsloth + SGLang #21419 / #23134 + llama.cpp #20969 stack. The arxiv wave is broadening the alternatives (which the prediction explicitly named as "TurboQuant + ≥1 alternative"), and Foundry Local remains the missing Windows-default integration point.

The Anthropic Opus 4.7 GA listing across Claude products + API + Bedrock + Vertex AI + Microsoft Foundry (carried in today's report) is the cleanest match for the 2026-05-01 multi-cloud-frontier-model SLA prediction: a single non-OpenAI foundation lab now ships across all four major hosting paths, which is the "frontier on every cloud" pattern the prediction calls out as the white-space the Apr 27 Microsoft-license-non-exclusive reset opened.

The Pentagon-Anthropic split + 7-vendor classified-network deal is partial confirmation of the 2026-05-01 hyperscaler-AI-lab capital-coupling prediction at the **procurement-precedent** layer rather than the contract-restructuring layer; the federal-judge-blocked Trump freeze + Susie Wiles / Amodei meeting on Apr 17 sets the deal-or-no-deal binary the prediction expects to force every frontier-lab board to formally adopt either the carve-out or the full-DoD-use posture by Q3 earnings.

The single explicit correction in today's report — Apr Jobs Report releases **May 8**, not May 1 — partially invalidates the framing of the 2026-05-01 Powell-Warsh-handoff prediction. The Powell-on-Board-through-2028 and Warsh-Senate-Banking-confirmation legs hold; the "May 1 Apr-jobs print at front of handoff window" leg does not. This is the one rolling-forecast prediction that needs a date-shift in next-day write.

Two predictions show only weak confirmation today: the AGI-clause-unwind-template prediction (2026-04-28) saw no new contract restructuring, only the Apr 27 Microsoft–OpenAI carry; the US-state-statutory-identity prediction (2026-04-27) has scaffolding building (CSA Identity, Microsoft Agent 365 + Entra) but no state-legislation signal. Both remain plausible on their stated H2 / Q3 2026 horizons.

Dormant pool re-check: no entries are due (the only entry, 20260420-3 "Headless Everything," has Next ping 2026-05-11). Layer 1 keyword scan + Layer 2 semantic scan produced one revived candidate (20260420-3) on mixed-direction signal — Simon Willison continues weblog activity (lightweight HTML, not headless), WindowsWorld is research investment in GUI agents (counter-evidence to "screen-less UI"), Microsoft Agent 365's "agent inventory" surface is Agent-Registry-adjacent (mildly supportive). Honest relevance is 2.
relation_to_my_preds:
Strong overlap with two of your three standing predictions today.

Your **malicious-local-LLMs / zero-trust-as-fundamental-safeguard** prediction is directly tracked by today's news. Microsoft Agent 365 GA puts the "intentionally designed system paths in which AI alone cannot access data" architecture into mainstream enterprise pricing — observe / govern / secure pillars + Entra ID + Purview + Intune + Defender + per-tenant MCP-server allow/block. The companion CSA blog "Identity in the Age of AI: Rethinking Zero Trust's First Pillar" (May 1) explicitly argues identity is the operative first pillar of zero trust for AI agents, which is the framing your prediction calls out. The behavior-monitoring side — which your prediction notes will draw short-term attention but does not overturn the bypass risk — is also visible today as the second pillar of Agent 365's "observe" surface (agent inventory + observability dashboards + policy templates) and through CSAI Foundation's STAR-for-AI Catastrophic Risk Annex (4-phase rollout June 2026 → Dec 2027). The path your prediction says will be stable in both short and long term — zero-trust-based access control for AI — is the one Microsoft is anchoring at $15/user/mo. The path your prediction says will attract attention only short term — behavior monitoring/management — is also shipping but as a layer on top, not as the substrate. Today's news is congruent with the structural bet your prediction makes.

Your **cloud-for-advanced + local-for-routine + cloud SaaS prices rise** prediction is partially tracked. The local-LLM stack continues to mature today (DeepSeek V4 Pro/Flash with 1M context, Hybrid Attention, Huawei Ascend optimization at $0.14/$0.28 per M tokens for V4 Flash; Qwen 3.6; Kimi K2.6 first open-weight to beat GPT-5.4 (xhigh) on SWE-Bench Pro; GLM-5.1 leading SWE-Bench Pro; Bonsai-8B 1-bit on iPhone 17 Pro Max at 44 tok/s; Foundry Local; Unsloth 2026.4.8) — all of which is the "routine daily operations handled by local LLMs" leg. The cloud SaaS-price-rise leg has weaker direct evidence today: the data points are Microsoft Agent 365 priced at $15/user/mo (a *new* per-user line layered on top of M365, not a price increase to an existing service), AAPL Services $30.97B (+16%) (services pricing power on the consumer side), and the $700B 2026 AI-capex tape (which is the cost-pressure precondition your prediction names, but not yet the service-delivery-model / pricing-structure change you forecast as the first appearance form). The capex-vs-electricity-cost squeeze is building; the pricing-structure shift you predict is not yet visible in today's news.

Your **RL / LLM-based approaches improve forecasting performance for real-world phenomena** prediction has thinner direct support today. The closest analogue is arxiv 2604.20801 (Synthesizing Multi-Agent Harnesses for Vulnerability Discovery), which composes multi-agent harnesses to discover 10 zero-day Chrome vulnerabilities including CVE-2026-5280 + CVE-2026-6297 — that is LLM-driven prediction-of-vulnerabilities-in-real-systems, which is in the spirit of your prediction even though the target is security rather than open-domain forecasting. The ICLR 2026 outstanding-papers wave (Q-RAG multi-step retrieval via RL-trained embedders, MedAgentGym agentic biomedical training, "LLMs Get Lost In Multi-Turn Conversation") is research-side evidence that the LLM-agent-improves-via-RL pattern is mainstreaming in 2026, but no scheduled-task-style forecasting benchmark publication landed today.
